workflow.name: Generate and post Apple App Store review replies with Anthropic Claude
workflow.description:
## Generate and post Apple App Store review replies with Anthropic Claude, Google Drive and App Store Connect API 
This workflow empowers app developers and community management teams by automating the generation and posting of responses to user reviews on the Apple App Store. Designed to streamline the engagement process, it drastically reduces the manual workload on community managers by integrating AI-driven responses with necessary human oversight. By leveraging n8n's workflow automation capabilities, this solution eliminates the need for costly third-party platforms like AppFollow or Appbot, making it a cost-effective and efficient alternative.

**Pre-requisites**
* Google Drive & Google Sheets access: To store and manage review spreadsheets.
* App Store Connect API credentials: To fetch and respond to app reviews.
* LLM credentials (e.g., Anthropic): Required for generating responses.
* Slack account

### Workflow steps
**1. Initialise and trigger workflow:** The process begins daily at 10 AM through a scheduled trigger.

**2. Fetch application data:** Utilizes a data table (Apple App Store apps) to retrieve a list of applications with their app id and name, essential for identifying review sources.

**3. Collect App Store Reviews:** Retrieves previous day's reviews from the App Store Connect API based on app data. Stores the reviews in Google Sheets for further processing.

**4. Generate AI Responses:** AI model generates initial responses based on review content. Responses are structured and stored along with reviews within a Google Spreadsheet located in a Google Drive folder called *ToReview*. A Slack message is sent with the URL of the file

**5. Human Review & Modification:** Community managers review and refine AI-generated responses. Reviewed spreadsheets are moved to the *ToSubmit* Google Drive folder by the editor.

**6. Post Verified Responses:** Workflow triggers again at 5 PM to access reviewed spreadsheets in *ToSubmit* folder. It posts the human-verified responses back to the respective reviews on Apple App Store using App Store Connect API. Logs are maintained, recording each response's success or failure.

**7. Archive processed spreadsheets:** After posting the responses, workflow moves the processed files from *ToSubmit*  to a different folder called *Archived*

parameters.content:
## Generate and post Apple App Store review replies with Anthropic Claude, Google Drive and App Store Connect API 
This workflow empowers app developers and community management teams by automating the generation and posting of responses to user reviews on the Apple App Store. Designed to streamline the engagement process, it drastically reduces the manual workload on community managers by integrating AI-driven responses with necessary human oversight. By leveraging n8n's workflow automation capabilities, this solution eliminates the need for costly third-party platforms like AppFollow or Appbot, making it a cost-effective and efficient alternative.

**Pre-requisites**
* Google Drive & Google Sheets access: To store and manage review spreadsheets.
* App Store Connect API credentials: To fetch and respond to app reviews.
* LLM credentials (e.g., Anthropic): Required for generating responses.
* Slack account

### Workflow steps
**1. Initialise and trigger workflow:** The process begins daily at 10 AM through a scheduled trigger.

**2. Fetch application data:** Utilizes a data table (Apple App Store apps) to retrieve a list of applications with their app id and name, essential for identifying review sources.

**3. Collect App Store Reviews:** Retrieves previous day's reviews from the App Store Connect API based on app data. Stores the reviews in Google Sheets for further processing.

**4. Generate AI Responses:** AI model generates initial responses based on review content. Responses are structured and stored along with reviews within a Google Spreadsheet located in a Google Drive folder called *ToReview*. A Slack message is sent with the URL of the file

**5. Human Review & Modification:** Community managers review and refine AI-generated responses. Reviewed spreadsheets are moved to the *ToSubmit* Google Drive folder by the editor.

**6. Post Verified Responses:** Workflow triggers again at 5 PM to access reviewed spreadsheets in *ToSubmit* folder. It posts the human-verified responses back to the respective reviews on Apple App Store using App Store Connect API. Logs are maintained, recording each response's success or failure.

**7. Archive processed spreadsheets:** After posting the responses, workflow moves the processed files from *ToSubmit*  to a different folder called *Archived*

## FIRST STEP: GENERATE AI RESPONSES
At 10 am, download the previous day's reviews from Apple App Store and generate AI responses in a spreadsheet in *ToReview* folder. Send a Slack message with the url of the file

## SECOND STEP: HUMAN IN THE LOOP
A human reviews the spreadsheet, makes any required adjustments to the responses and moves the spreadsheet to a different folder called *ToSubmit*

## THIRD STEP: POST RESPONSES
At 5pm, fetch the spreadsheets in *ToSubmit* folder, post the responses using the Apple App Store Connect API, create execution logs and move the processed spreadsheets to a different folder called *Archived*
Post responses using App Store Connect API: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/appstoreconnectapi/post-v1-customerreviewresponses

### Data table fields
* reviewID: App Store Identifier for each review.
* app_id: Corresponding application's app id.
* successful: Boolean status indicating the posting success.

### Data table fields 
* app_id: Apple app id for each app. App id can be found in the Apple App Store url of the app. For example the app id of the app with url https://apps.apple.com/us/app/claude-by-anthropic/id6473753684 is 6473753684
* name: The application’s name (eg. Claude by Anthropic)
Collect all the reviews and responses in a spreadsheet, sorted by rating

parameters.content: Send the Google Drive url of the file in a Slack message

If there are no reviews for the app from yesterday, continue the loop with the next app

parameters.text:
=You are a helpful community manager of a mobile apps company and your job is to read the player reviews on Apple App Store and to generate a response to be posted on behalf of the company's community team.

Reviews have the following elements:

* Review id (reviewId): Apple App Store id of the review.
* Star rating (starRating): an integer from 1 to 5, 1 being the lowest rating and 5 being the highest.
* Title (title): Title of the review or feedback provided by the player.
* Body (body): Body of the review or feedback provided by the player. The lower the star rating the more likely that the review will be a negative one. 
* Apple App Store  id of the app (app_id).
* Name of the app (appName)

Follow these guidelines when generating the response for a review:

* Always start with thanking the user for sharing a review.
* If the review is a negative one, respond with an apologetic and empathetic tone. Let the user know that we take their feedback seriously and we'd like them to contact us in the app to discuss their feedback. These responses should be 5 sentences max.
* If the review is a positive one with 5 star rating, respond with a celebratory and grateful tone. These responses should be 2 sentences max.
* The total length of the responses should be less than 500 characters. 
* Do not use any quotation marks (", ' etc) in the responses you generate.

Here are some example responses for negative reviews that you can use to generate your responses:

<Add some examples here> 

The response you generate should always be in English, even if the language of the review is a different one. Do not generate a response in any language other than English. 

Always generate a list of customer review responses in JSON format. Each item should contain the following fields:
- app_id: The Apple id of the application.
- reviewId: A unique identifier for the review.
- title: The title of the review submitted by the user.
- body: The body of the review submitted by the user.
- starRating: An integer representing the star rating (1-5).
- response: The generated response to the review.

Example format:
[
  {
    "app_id": "6608975929",
    "app_name": "App Name1",
    "reviewId": "64f8b751-7fba-43bd-99re-542693b6c01d",
    "title": "Highly recommend!",
    "body": "Love this app so much!",
    "starRating": "5",
    "response": "Thank you for your positive feedback."
  },
  {
    "app_id": "5508975929",
    "app_name": "App Name2t",
    "reviewId": "b7a7b009-5086-441d-9635-27239bd54c19",
    "title": "Wouldn't recommend!",
    "body": "Full of ads!",
    "starRating": "2",
    "response": "We're sorry for the inconvenience. Please contact us to discuss further."
  }
]

Ensure all generated responses conform to this format.

Here are the list of reviews you should generate responses for. Generate a different response for each review and include all reviews and responses in your output:

{{ $json.toJsonString() }}

parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
[

    "body": "Love this app so much!",
    "starRating": "5",
    "response": "Thank you for your positive feedback."
  },
  {
    "app_id": "6608975929",
    "app_name": "App Name2",
    "reviewId": "b7a7b+1234456790d-9635-27239be45c19",
    "title": "Wouldn't recommend!",
    "body": "Full of ads!",
    "starRating": "2",
    "response": "We're sorry for the inconvenience. Please contact us to discuss further."
  }
]

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
